entries.garrytan/gbrain: This opinionated brain provides 25 specialized skills for autonomous data ingestion and enrichment alongside over 30 MCP tools. It utilizes PGLite for zero-config local storage, combining vector and relational data to serve as a robust foundation for Hermes Agent integrations.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: Hindsight implements biomimetic data structures to facilitate long-term learning through dedicated retain, recall, and reflect workflows. It offers a simple two-line LLM wrapper that automates memory management, allowing agents to evolve their mental models over time.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: This recursive context harness uses a multi-agent loop to extract and persist validated knowledge across eleven distinct scenario families. It is particularly effective for distilling stable agent behaviors into local models via MLX integration, ensuring success in complex or fragile tool-based tasks.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: This plugin introduces a lossless context engine that uses immutable SQLite storage and a hierarchical DAG structure to ensure no conversation data is ever deleted. It provides built-in tools for searching and expanding compacted context, enabling multi-level summary escalation for long-running sessions.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: Honcho provides a private, self-hosted backend for cross-session memory that supports any OpenAI-compatible provider or local inference server. The included automated setup script simplifies the deployment of Docker, PostgreSQL, and Redis for a secure memory infrastructure.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: ClawMem is a local-first context engine that eliminates cloud dependencies by using a hybrid retrieval system combining vector search, BM25, and multi-graph traversal. It enables seamless memory sharing between Hermes, OpenClaw, and Claude Code without requiring external API keys.
entries.plur-ai/plur: This shared memory layer stores persistent data as open-format YAML engrams, utilizing a hybrid retrieval system with zero API costs. It features a unique biological approach where memories automatically decay or strengthen based on their usage frequency within multi-agent systems.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: Developed for the Hermes 2026 Hackathon, this project features an anticipatory cache that prefetches context into a local intuition file. It leverages SQLite-vec and node-llama-cpp to provide a privacy-focused, local-first memory solution via an MCP server.

entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: This library provides 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to major frameworks like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST. It equips Hermes with specialized domain knowledge across 26 security domains, making it a powerhouse for defensive and offensive security tasks.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: This skill audits and rewrites content to eliminate 36 distinct AI writing patterns and structural tells. By using a three-tier word replacement system and a second-pass audit, it ensures Hermes produces more natural, human-like prose.
entries.wondelai/skills: A modular, cross-platform library that brings standardized frameworks for architecture, UX design, and product strategy to Hermes. It utilizes a marketplace and CLI for easy installation, allowing agents to adopt professional-grade workflows instantly.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: This project implements the Agent Skills open specification for Pydantic AI with a focus on efficiency. It uses progressive disclosure of metadata to reduce token usage while supporting both filesystem-based and programmatic skill definitions.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: Hermes can generate editable .drawio diagrams from natural language descriptions using this skill, which includes presets for ML models and tensor shapes. It features an automated self-check loop to identify and fix layout issues, ensuring high-quality visual outputs.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: This skill enables Hermes to interact with the Chainlink Runtime Environment and CCIP workflows for cross-chain smart contract operations. It follows the agentskills.io specification to provide standardized methods for contract discovery and monitoring.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: A literate programming tool that allows Hermes to weave code and documentation together into .lit.md files. It includes a tangler to extract runnable source files and automatically syncs edits back to the primary document.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: This plugin introduces cross-instance shared memory via markdown-based directories, allowing Hermes to remember past work. It includes an end-to-end pipeline for extracting decision-quality data to train and evaluate replacement models.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: A registry and SDK for discovering and managing reusable AI skills with support for recursive dependency resolution. It integrates commerce features via TON and Stripe, enabling a marketplace for monetizing specialized agent capabilities.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: The official skill set for FLUX image generation, providing Hermes with standardized prompting guidelines and API integration patterns. It supports precise hex color specification and JSON structured prompting for high-fidelity visual synthesis.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: This meta-skill watches active workflows to automatically detect and propose new, reusable skills. It generates both documentation and Python scaffolds, allowing Hermes to effectively expand its own capability set during execution.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: A harness for long-running agents that maintains shared state on disk and facilitates context transfers via structured UKI v5.4 handoffs. It provides a TUI and JSON snapshots to track complex missions across multiple agent instances.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: This skill enables Hermes to execute complex, multi-step tasks autonomously with built-in checkpoints for progress tracking. It includes rollback procedures to ensure safe recovery if a task encounters an error during execution.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: A progressive MCP client that uses three-layer lazy loading to minimize token usage for tool definitions. It maintains persistent connections to multiple MCP servers via a socket-based daemon for efficient resource management.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: This tool converts repositories and developer discourse into agentskills.io-compliant skills using AST analysis for verified function signatures. It links every AI instruction to specific source file lines to ensure high-fidelity code generation.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: A community hub for browsing and searching Hermes skills with one-click installation and automated security scanning. It protects users from malicious payloads while supporting a creator marketplace through micropayment protocols.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: This project brings the agentskills.io specification to the C# and .NET ecosystem, including MCP resource templates for on-demand loading. It provides vendor-specific shortcuts to streamline skill integration for developers using the .NET stack.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides a comprehensive suite of Nix packages for terminal-based AI coding agents, including Hermes. It automates daily updates for all included tools and supports a mix of open-source and proprietary binary-based development agents.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: Hermesclaw focuses on security by providing hardware-level enforcement of network, filesystem, and process boundaries for the agent. It features three security policy presets and includes secure gateways for integrating Hermes with Telegram, Discord, and Slack.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This repository offers a NixOS module for declarative management of Hermes configurations, documents, and systemd services. It prioritizes security through sops-nix integration and supports a hybrid skill model that combines Nix-managed assets with interactive CLI installations.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: Designed for Windows users, this no-install desktop application provides a GUI and over 100 tools without requiring admin rights or Docker. It features integrated GPU monitoring, local model management, and a workflow engine for scheduled automation.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This project provides a streamlined Docker sandbox that includes both the official Hermes installation and mini-swe-agent. It supports persistent state through mounted directories and allows users to build specific Hermes versions using build arguments.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Targeting production environments, this deployment configures the Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service for Ubuntu and Debian. It is optimized for headless operation and enables the autonomous execution of recurring AI cron jobs.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This Docker Compose stack integrates with Portainer to deploy Hermes alongside a ttyd web terminal interface. It simplifies the deployment lifecycle by automating image builds through GitHub Actions and the GitHub Container Registry.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: This self-hosted orchestration platform manages multi-agent workflows using a lightweight SQLite backend with zero external dependencies. It is ideal for Hermes users who require real-time task monitoring and strict quality control through the built-in Aegis review system.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: Swarmclaw provides a self-hosted runtime for building autonomous agent swarms that support over 20 providers, including local Ollama and Hermes. It distinguishes itself by enabling agents to learn new skills from conversations and maintain persistent memory across delegated tasks.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: This framework focuses on meta-optimization by using an automated outer-loop search to refine the harness code used for Hermes Agent evaluations. It orchestrates complex benchmarks like TBLite and TB2, tracking performance frontiers through structured candidate mutation.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: Gladiator creates a competitive arena where two autonomous AI companies, powered by Hermes Agent, compete for GitHub stars. The system orchestrates nine specialized agents and visualizes their real-time execution, code generation, and scoreboard metrics on a live dashboard.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: This project coordinates a massive pipeline of 17 specialized agents dedicated to tasks ranging from infrastructure to security. It enforces rigorous quality gates by routing all outputs through mandatory reviewer and tester agent loops to ensure production-ready code.
entries.supermodeltools/bigiron: Bigiron implements an AI-native software development lifecycle by providing Hermes with structural code graphs via the Supermodel MCP. It automates an eight-phase development process, using graph-diff gates to ensure architectural integrity at every step from planning to health checks.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: This framework utilizes ACP-style delegation to route tasks between Hermes subagents and external models like Codex or Claude Code. It prioritizes operational safety by isolating delegation workflows to prevent context pollution and enforcing strict timeouts and output limits.

entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: This Rust-based TUI tracks token consumption and real-time costs across over 20 AI coding agents, including Hermes and Claude Code. It utilizes LiteLLM pricing data to provide developers with precise financial oversight and 3D usage visualizations for their agentic workflows.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: This customization tool allows users to personalize the Hermes terminal interface through YAML-defined themes and inheritance. It modifies visual elements like banners, spinners, and response boxes with community-driven styles ranging from Cyberdyne Systems to Vault-Tec.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: This project ports the Hermes Agent brain and its 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel for native execution without external processes or OAuth. It specifically enhances the environment with MLOps tools for vLLM inference, GGUF management, and reinforcement learning training.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: An AI-native browser designed for autonomous control, this tool provides an MCP-native interface for external harnesses like Hermes Agent. It features durable session persistence for agent stability and a human-in-the-loop supervisory layer for real-time transcript monitoring and approvals.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: This framework enables the creation of scoped Hermes Agents that are restricted to specific project folders with local configurations and memories. It allows developers to seamlessly toggle between global and project-local agents, each equipped with unique tools and custom instructions.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: A static analysis tool that validates agent configurations, tool descriptions, and system prompts without incurring LLM costs or latency. It uses seven structural detectors to identify common failure modes and provides CI-ready gating based on HERM v1.1 scoring.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: This migration utility streamlines the transition from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent by mapping complex model configurations and personas to the Hermes YAML format. It automates the transfer of memories, skills, and workspace files while securely handling environment-level credentials.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: This deployment script uses Docker to orchestrate a full Hermes Agent stack including LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant in minutes. It comes pre-configured with 29 community plugins and automated routing for free models and local GPU inference.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: This no-install Windows desktop application provides a GUI for Hermes Agent that requires no administrative rights or Docker setup. It features over 100 integrated tools for GPU monitoring and local model management alongside a workflow engine for scheduled automation.

entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: This lightweight web interface offers full feature parity with the Hermes CLI through a streamlined three-panel layout for chat, sessions, and file browsing. Built with Python and vanilla JavaScript, it provides a responsive, mobile-friendly experience without the complexity of modern build tools.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: A comprehensive native web workspace that unifies chat, terminal access, and memory browsing into a single interface. It features real-time SSE streaming for visual tool-call rendering and supports diverse local backends including Ollama and vLLM.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: This self-hosted dashboard functions as a multi-agent gateway, allowing users to configure profiles and monitor system metrics in real-time. It stands out for its granular security, offering role-based access control with 28 distinct permissions and detailed token cost analytics.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: A native macOS workspace that provides direct SSH connectivity to Hermes without requiring local file mirroring or background daemons. It includes a multi-tabbed terminal with live theme controls and a profile-aware interface for managing remote sessions and cron jobs.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: This desktop companion simplifies the initial setup of Hermes Agent through guided installation and dependency resolution. Beyond local chat, it serves as a configuration hub for connecting agents to messaging platforms like Discord and Telegram.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: Focused on observability, this dashboard provides real-time monitoring of session activity, gateway status, and operational costs. It enables deep inspection of agent history through full-text SQLite search and a read-only view of scheduled cron jobs.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: A self-hosted workspace that emphasizes agent isolation and extensibility through a built-in marketplace for skills and MCP servers. It features an automated setup wizard that supports daemon and systemd execution for persistent background operation.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: This glassmorphic web interface utilizes a unique single-file React architecture that requires no build steps or external dependencies. It provides real-time log streaming and tool-call visualization alongside an integrated memory inspector for rapid agent configuration.
